sprint: Unified Layer Ordering — Moveable World-Owned Shapes Join a Reorderable Group Too
task: Same-day follow-up to the Unified Layer Ordering feature: the user asked 'ok so to test do i need to reauthor or just refresh the creator', then reported a real live World ('My shapes' -- Background/Builder Ba.../First BG/Apple) where 'reordering in object strip not happening.' Root cause, confirmed via code reading: on that exact page, the two non-Apple objects are both moveable, non-overlay ('slide'-scoped) World-owned Decoration Shapes -- the prior sprint's own disclosed scope cut excluded ALL World-owned objects from the reorderable group, so with only the Apple sticker actually draggable and nothing else wired for drag/drop, dropping it anywhere was structurally a no-op. Fixed with a deliberately narrower extension than full cross-bucket reordering (an AskUserQuestion call failed with a transient AbortError; proceeded with the lower-risk 'Recommended' default rather than block, disclosed in CLAUDE.md): a moveable:true Layer Pack object now joins a reorderable group of its own, strictly WITHIN its own draw bucket -- 'earlier' (non-overlay World objects), 'story' (the Scene-element/Sticker pass), 'overlay' (overlay-scoped World objects), always drawn in that fixed relative order. Two objects sharing a bucket can now be freely reordered relative to each other; crossing a bucket boundary is still not attempted -- that remains the deferred, bigger two-pass-drawing change already named in the prior sprint. New _applyOrderOverride(natural,order) in renderer/slideRenderer.js is the one resolution step both the Story bucket and the two World buckets now share against the same page-wide layerOrder array; getReorderableIds(s) returns the union of all three buckets' already-resolved (not just natural) ids -- a real bug caught and fixed mid-implementation via the new test, where the World-bucket helper initially only ever returned natural push order, never reflecting a just-completed reorder. New SlideRenderer.getReorderBucket(s,id) reports which bucket an id belongs to; js/objectStrip.js's drag handler uses it to refuse a cross-bucket drop outright -- no drop-indicator, no reorder -- rather than silently accepting a drag that would move nothing. Verified via a new Playwright reproduction rebuilding the exact reported page shape (two moveable non-overlay Decoration Shapes + one Sticker): both Shapes confirmed reorderable and sharing the 'earlier' bucket; exactly 3 real draggable cards; a real drag swaps the two Shapes' resolved draw order; a cross-bucket drag (Sticker onto a Shape) is correctly refused. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (19 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
